meta.title: XplurData Docs
meta.description: Full documentation for XplurData — the self-hosted observability platform.

hero.headline: Full Observability.
hero.description: XplurData is a self-hosted, Docker-based observability platform built for teams who want full control over their telemetry — no per-seat pricing, no data leaving your infrastructure.

pillars.title: Ingest
pillars.desc: Receive logs, traces and metrics from any service via OpenTelemetry OTLP/HTTP on port 4318.

pillars.title: Store
pillars.desc: Apache Doris v4.0.4 — columnar analytical database purpose-built for telemetry at scale.

pillars.title: Explore
pillars.desc: XD-API + React UI with full-text search, filtering, and real-time queries at port 80.

pillars.title: Deploy
pillars.desc: 4 Docker containers. One install command. Runs on Linux, macOS or Windows in under 5 minutes.

explore_cards.title: Quick Install
explore_cards.desc: Install the full stack with one command on Linux, macOS, or Windows.

explore_cards.desc: How the four containers work together. Includes the full system diagram.

explore_cards.desc: Use telemetrygen, Python SDK or any OTel-compatible SDK to ship data.

quick-install.title: Quick Install
quick-install.subtitle: Get the full XplurData stack running with a single command.

sections.title: Docker Compose (Manual)
sections.code:
git clone https://github.com/xplurdata/xplurdata.git
cd xplurdata
docker compose up -d

sections.text: First boot takes 3–5 minutes while Apache Doris initialises. The Web UI will show a loading screen until the backend is ready.

XplurData is intentionally simple. Every component runs as a Docker container and communicates over an internal bridge network. There are no external dependencies — no managed databases, no cloud services, no API keys.
Images are loaded from the src path relative to docs.html. To add images to any page, add an "image" or "image-grid" section to docs.json — no code changes needed.

sections.title: Architecture Overview
sections.alt: XplurData architecture diagram showing the four Docker containers
sections.caption: XplurData four-container architecture — OTel Collector → Doris → FastAPI → React UI

sections.text: Only ports 80 and 4318 need to be exposed externally. All other inter-service communication happens on the internal Docker network.

images.caption: Log Explorer — filter, search and query logs
User Management — add and manage users

sending-logs.title: Sending Logs
sending-logs.subtitle: Ship telemetry from any language or tool to XplurData.

tabs.label: Python
tabs.code:
pip install opentelemetry-sdk opentelemetry-exporter-otlp

# instrument.py
from opentelemetry import trace
from opentelemetry.sdk.trace import TracerProvider
from opentelemetry.exporter.otlp.proto.http.trace_exporter import OTLPSpanExporter
from opentelemetry.sdk.trace.export import BatchSpanProcessor

provider = TracerProvider()
exporter = OTLPSpanExporter(
    endpoint="http://<YOUR_HOST>:4318/v1/traces"
)
provider.add_span_processor(BatchSpanProcessor(exporter))
trace.set_tracer_provider(provider)

tracer = trace.get_tracer("my-service")
with tracer.start_as_current_span("my-operation"):
    print("Hello, XplurData!")

tabs.label: Node.js
Java
tabs.code:
# Download the Java agent (zero-code instrumentation)
wget https://github.com/open-telemetry/opentelemetry-java-instrumentation\
  /releases/latest/download/opentelemetry-javaagent.jar

# Run your app with the agent
java -javaagent:opentelemetry-javaagent.jar \
  -Dotel.service.name=my-service \
  -Dotel.exporter.otlp.endpoint=http://<YOUR_HOST>:4318 \
  -jar app.jar

tabs.label: Go
tabs.code:
go get go.opentelemetry.io/otel \
       go.opentelemetry.io/otel/exporters/otlp/otlptrace/otlptracehttp

// main.go
exp, _ := otlptracehttp.New(ctx,
    otlptracehttp.WithEndpoint("<YOUR_HOST>:4318"),
    otlptracehttp.WithInsecure(),
)
tp := trace.NewTracerProvider(
    trace.WithBatcher(exp),
    trace.WithResource(resource.NewWithAttributes(
        semconv.SchemaURL,
        semconv.ServiceNameKey.String("my-service"),
    )),
)
otel.SetTracerProvider(tp)

tabs.label: telemetrygen
tabs.code:
# Install telemetrygen
go install github.com/open-telemetry/opentelemetry-collector-contrib/cmd/telemetrygen@latest

# Send 1000 log records
telemetrygen logs \
  --otlp-endpoint <YOUR_HOST>:4318 \
  --otlp-insecure \
  --duration 10s \
  --rate 100

# Send traces
telemetrygen traces \
  --otlp-endpoint <YOUR_HOST>:4318 \
  --otlp-insecure \
  --duration 10s

sections.text: Replace <YOUR_HOST> with your server's IP address or hostname. Use port 4318 for HTTP and 4317 for gRPC.

management.title: Management
management.subtitle: Day-to-day operations for your XplurData stack.

sections.title: Check Stack Status
sections.code:
docker compose ps

# Expected output:
# NAME              STATUS         PORTS
# doris-fe          Up 2 hours     0.0.0.0:9030->9030/tcp
# doris-be          Up 2 hours
# otel-collector    Up 2 hours     0.0.0.0:4318->4318/tcp
# xplurdata-app     Up 2 hours     0.0.0.0:80->80/tcp

sections.title: Stream Live Logs
sections.code:
# All containers
docker compose logs -f

# Specific container
docker compose logs -f xplurdata-app
docker compose logs -f otel-collector

sections.title: Restart Services
sections.code:
# Restart all
docker compose restart

# Restart specific service
docker compose restart xplurdata-app

sections.title: Update to Latest Version
sections.code:
docker compose pull
docker compose up -d

sections.title: Full Uninstall
sections.code:
# Stop and remove containers + volumes (WARNING: deletes all data)
docker compose down -v

# Also remove images
docker compose down -v --rmi all

sections.text: Running docker compose down -v will permanently delete all telemetry data stored in Doris. Back up your data first if needed.

troubleshooting.title: Troubleshooting
troubleshooting.subtitle: Common issues and how to resolve them.

items.q: Web UI shows a blank page or loading spinner indefinitely
items.a: Doris takes 3–5 minutes to fully initialise on first boot. Wait and refresh. If it persists beyond 10 minutes, check Doris FE logs: docker compose logs doris-fe

items.q: OTLP data not appearing in the UI
items.a: Verify the collector is receiving data: docker compose logs otel-collector. Check that your SDK endpoint is http://<host>:4318/v1/logs (not 4317 unless using gRPC). Ensure no firewall is blocking port 4318.
Edit docker-compose.yml and change the host port mapping. For example, change "80:80" to "8080:80" and access the UI at port 8080.
Doris BE requires at least 4 GB RAM and vm.max_map_count >= 2000000. Run: sudo sysctl -w vm.max_map_count=2000000
You may have Docker Compose v1 installed. Try docker-compose (with hyphen) instead, or upgrade to Docker Desktop which includes Compose v2.
Doris FE exposes a MySQL-compatible interface on port 9030. Connect with: mysql -h 127.0.0.1 -P 9030 -u root
